sections.paragraph:
Pl. אֶשְׁכְּלֹות and אַשְׁכְּלֹות (as if from אֶשְׁכֹּלֶת, comp. אַרְמֹון), m. Num 13:23.

(1) a cluster, Traubenkamm [“prop. the stem or stalk of a cluster; Lat. racemus.”], whence berries, or flowers, which hang in clusters like grapes; as of dates, Son 7:8 of flowers of the henna, Son 1:14 especially of the vine, either with the addition of הַגֶּפֶן Son 7:9, עֳנָבִים Num 13:23, 24 Num 13:24or absol. Isa 65:8 Mic 7:1. Once, Gen 40:10, אֶשְׁכֹּל is distinguished from עֵנָב grape, and denotes the stem, racemus, pr. so called. The words הִבְשִׁילוּ אַשְׁכְּלֹותֶיהָ עֲנָבִים should be rendered “and its (the vine’s) stems (racemes) brought forth grapes.” Germ. die Rebenkämme trieben reife Trauben od. reife Beeren. To this answers the Arab. إِثْكاَلُ, عِثْكاَلُ palm branch; Eth. አስካል፡ grape, vine; whence the verb ሰከለ፡ to bear grapes; Syr. and Ch. ܣܓܘܽܥܳ סְגֹולָא grape, stem, cluster. In such a variety of orthography the etymology is doubtful. Perhaps אֶשְׁכֹּל may be for אֶשְׂכֹּל, from שָׂכַל, شكل to bind, to plait, as a plaiting, braid of grapes. Compare עֵנָב.

(2) [Eshcol], pr.n.-(a) of a valley abounding in vines in the southern part of the Holy Land, Num 13:23, 24 Num 13:24 32:9 Deu 1:24.-(b) m. Gen 14:13, 24 Gen 14:24.
